name: Geri Allen
abstract: Geri Antoinette Allen (June 12, 1957 – June 27, 2017) was an American jazz pianist, composer, and educator. She taught at the University of Michigan and the University of Pittsburgh.

about_quotes.speaker: Sands
about_quotes.evidence: What did you learn from her? ... Her and Wynton Marsalis actually taught me how to really study music.

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: I just knew him, because he died quite a few years ago... I think the most I’ve heard about Herbie Nichols is that Geri Allen made a point of playing a lot of his music.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Tim Ries
about_quotes.evidence: Marcus Belgrave who kind of was like helped oh like Kenny Garrett, Geri Allen, Robert Hurst, a lot of people played with him
about_quotes.doc_title: Tim Ries interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, March 19, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Vi Redd
about_quotes.evidence: Oh I love his playing. And Geri Allen. They are performing well, it’s just that there’s not enough of it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Vi Redd interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 13, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Burrill Crohn
about_quotes.evidence: the woman piano player, Allen, Geri Allen playing Mary Lou Williams
about_quotes.doc_title: Burrill Crohn interviewed by Monk Rowe, Woodstock, New York, January 3, 2012
